title: Compressed Sparse Attention
description: A DeepSeek-V4 attention variant that mixes compression and sparse access so very long prompts stay tractable.
openingSummary: Dense long-context attention becomes too expensive when every query wants every detailed key and value. Compressed sparse attention lowers that cost by keeping a compact memory view and only paying for selected detailed access.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: This is an attention design for extremely long contexts. The key idea is not only sparsity and not only compression. It is the combination: a compact representation for broad coverage plus selective detailed retrieval where it matters.

whyItExists.title: Why It Exists
whyItExists.body: The module targets KV-cache size, memory bandwidth, and long-context attention cost.

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.body: Tokens are first summarized into a compressed memory path. The query can then use sparse detailed access rather than reopening the full dense history every time.

mathOrComputeSchema.title: Math Or Compute Schema
mathOrComputeSchema.body: The sketch below emphasizes the two-stage idea: compact memory first, selective detailed attention second.

comparedToNearbyModules.title: Compared To Nearby Modules
comparedToNearbyModules.body: Sliding-window attention cuts reach. Sparse attention cuts connectivity. CSA instead keeps broad reach but changes how much detail the model stores and revisits.

exampleArchitectures.title: Example Architectures
exampleArchitectures.body: DeepSeek-V4 uses CSA as part of its million-token context story.

limitationsAndTradeoffs.title: Limitations And Tradeoffs
limitationsAndTradeoffs.body: Compression can hide detail if the summary path is too aggressive, and sparse access adds policy complexity about what deserves high-resolution lookup.

whyItStillMatters.title: Why It Still Matters
whyItStillMatters.body: It is a useful reference point for long-context design because it shows a third path beyond pure locality and pure dense recurrence.

computeFlow.alt: A flow from raw token history into compressed memory and then into sparse detailed access.

ktilde.definition: compressed keys and values that summarize broad context

qt.definition: the current query at time step t

as.definition: the sparse attention computation over compressed and selected detailed memory
